sprint: Magic Card Home -- One Constellation, Not Two
task: Fixes a real, user-reported bug: 'my card is showing 2 constellations why?' Traced to the previous sprint's own security fix -- Magic Card Home rendered a separate ambient decorative sky (decorativeSkyFor's safe stand-in pattern) ABOVE the reveal-gated card view, whose Back face shows the REAL constellation once tapped. Two genuinely different connect-the-dot star shapes on one screen, both looking like 'the' constellation -- a real violation of the platform's own founding 'one constellation, two faces' design principle. Given three fix options, the user chose: 'show me both sides of the identity card first.' js/magicCardUI.js's openHome() no longer builds a standalone ambient sky at all -- the reveal-gated two-sided card view (Front/Back canvases, same one the Awakening ceremony's First Claimed Moment already uses) is now Home's own primary, leading content, shown immediately with no toggle button needed. The Back face's reveal-gate (blur + Tap to Reveal) is completely unchanged -- nothing secret is exposed by default, only what leads the screen changed. MagicCard.growthSignals() and the companion-star rendering option are left in place, unused for now, per this codebase's own 'disclose the gap, do not delete the capability' precedent. Verified via a new 8-assertion Playwright suite confirming the ambient sky is gone, the card renders immediately, zero SVG constellation elements remain inside the Home panel (the header badge's own separate ambient sky, elsewhere on screen, is correctly unaffected), and the reveal-gate still works correctly. Two pre-existing suites' stale assertions against the removed structure were updated to test the same underlying properties through the new, correct structure. Full regression across every Magic Card Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged.
